title: Checklist evidence remains active
recommendation: Carry forward until checklist evidence and playbook-count drift are repaired.

title: Singular advisor skill-root defaults remain in live rebuild and scan surfaces
recommendation: Migrate skill_graph_scan, advisor_rebuild, tests, schemas, feature catalog, and dist outputs to .opencode/skills; then add a broader singular-root guard that catches nested .opencode/skill directories where relevant.

title: scripts/dist still contains stale singular .opencode/skill references
recommendation: Carry forward until generated scripts/dist references are rebuilt or intentionally patched.

title: 4-runtime agent mirror bodies remain out of parity
recommendation: Regenerate or manually reconcile runtime mirrors so body text is aligned except explicitly allowed runtime-specific metadata/frontmatter.

title: The new 093 playbooks are not reachable from their owning skill files
recommendation: Carry forward until the playbook roots are linked from owning SKILL.md files.

title: spec_folder is interpolated into executable workflow commands before containment is enforced
recommendation: Carry forward until spec-folder validation is centralized and all workflow command/writer paths consume only a sanitized repo-local specs path.

title: audit_descriptions.py still passes the zero-inventory stub case that 098/006 requires to fail
claim: The phase accepted a deferred zero-coverage guard even though REQ-003 makes the guard required for completion.
counterevidenceSought: A CLI flag or wrapper used by the acceptance test that converts zero audited items into a non-zero exit
alternativeExplanation: The implementation may have treated zero-coverage failure as advisory after narrowing scope, but the spec and success criteria still list it as required.
downgradeTrigger: Either implement the default non-zero zero-inventory guard and capture evidence, or update the spec/checklist to explicitly remove REQ-003 before claiming the phase resolved it.
recommendation: Add a default non-zero exit for zero audited items; make any intentionally-empty mode explicit with a flag.

title: Smart-router validation false-fails valid shared CLI router references
claim: The validator's hard failure is no longer a real missing-resource signal for the CLI shared-router docs.
counterevidenceSought: A documented contract requiring every referenced references/*.md path to be physically duplicated inside the referring skill
A CI invocation that excludes cross-skill shared references before running the validator
alternativeExplanation: The CLI skills may be intentionally depending on local copies that were never created; the checked-in links point at system-spec-kit, so the validator still disagrees with the authored reference model.
downgradeTrigger: Add cross-skill resolution support or local copies with updated links, then show check-smart-router.sh --json exits 0 while the zero-skill-root smoke still exits 1.
recommendation: Resolve markdown links with parent segments or add an explicit shared-resource allowlist/schema before classifying paths as missing.

title: Resolver containment remains insufficient as a standalone helper
recommendation: Resolve through P1-019 by making the resolver reject absolute paths, parent traversal, unresolved placeholders, and paths outside approved specs roots.

title: Prior P2 carried from earlier review state
recommendation: Retain until the prior registry entry is resolved or downgraded by a targeted pass.

title: Copilot target-authority guard is still non-functional in the imported module
recommendation: Implement and export buildCopilotPromptArg/validateSpecFolder in source and dist, or remove the Copilot branch's authority-guard claims until the helper exists and is tested.

title: Singular schema/default text remains beyond code-graph policy
recommendation: Carry forward until singular root text in source schemas and generated dist references is resolved.
